response:
I'll identify cell populations in slice 0 using scANVI transfer learning with the provided reference dataset. I'll follow the established workflow to annotate cell types.

```python
import scanpy as sc
from pathlib import Path
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('agg')  # Use non-interactive backend
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from celltype_scvi.annotation_scvi import annotate_celltype_scvi

# Get the target slice
slice_0 = session.get_slice(0)

print("="*60)
print("STAGE 1: Load and Validate")
print("="*60)

# Load reference data from the provided path
reference_path = '/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results_ablation/DeepSeek-V3.2/Q04/run_0/breast_cancer_reference.h5ad'
if not Path(reference_path).exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"Reference file not found: {reference_path}")

ref_adata = sc.read_h5ad(reference_path)
print(f"✓ Reference loaded: {ref_adata.n_obs:,} cells, {ref_adata.n_vars:,} genes")

# Validate reference has the specified celltype column
label_key = 'celltype'
if label_key not in ref_adata.obs.columns:
    raise ValueError(
        f"Reference does not have '{label_key}' column. "
        f"Available columns: {list(ref_adata.obs.columns)}"
    )

print(f"✓ Spatial data: {slice_0.adata.n_obs:,} cells, {slice_0.adata.n_vars:,} genes")
print(f"✓ Reference cell types: {ref_adata.obs[label_key].nunique()}")
print(f"✓ Reference cell type examples: {ref_adata.obs[label_key].unique()[:10].tolist()}")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("STAGE 2: scANVI Annotation")
print("="*60)

try:
    celltype_predictions = annotate_celltype_scvi(
        adata_spatial=slice_0.adata,
        adata_reference=ref_adata,
        label_key=label_key,
        n_latent=30,
        max_epochs_scvi=None,
        max_epochs_scanvi=20
    )

    print(f"\n✓ Annotation completed!")
    print(f"  Predicted {celltype_predictions.nunique()} cell types")

    # Show distribution
    print(f"\n  Cell type distribution:")
    counts = celltype_predictions.value_counts()
    for celltype, count in counts.head(15).items():
        print(f"    {celltype}: {count:,} cells ({count/len(celltype_predictions):.1%})")
    if len(counts) > 15:
        print(f"    ... and {len(counts)-15} more cell types")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"\n❌ Annotation failed: {e}")
    raise

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("STAGE 3: Storing Results")
print("="*60)

# Direct assignment - indices match
slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'] = celltype_predictions

print(f"✓ Added 'celltype' column to slice_0.adata.obs")
print(f"  Shape: {slice_0.adata.shape}")
print(f"  Celltype unique values: {slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'].nunique()}")

# Verify
assert 'celltype' in slice_0.adata.obs.columns, "Failed to add celltype column"
assert slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'].notna().all(), "Celltype contains NA values"

print("\n✓ Annotation complete! Celltype column added successfully.")
print("\nYou can now:")
print("  - Reload cell overlay in the web interface to see colored cells")
print("  - Analyze cell type distributions")
print("  - Compare cell types across ROIs")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("VISUALIZATION: Cell Type Distribution")
print("="*60)

# Get unique cell types and assign colors
unique_celltypes = slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'].unique()
n_types = len(unique_celltypes)

# Create a colormap
cmap = plt.cm.tab20
colors = {celltype: cmap(i % 20) for i, celltype in enumerate(unique_celltypes)}

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 10))

# Plot spatial distribution
for celltype in unique_celltypes:
    mask = slice_0.adata.obs['celltype'] == celltype
    plt.scatter(slice_0.adata.obs.loc[mask, 'x'], 
                slice_0.adata.obs.loc[mask, 'y'],
                c=[colors[celltype]], s=1, label=celltype, alpha=0.7)

plt.title(f'Cell Type Annotations on Slice 0\n{n_types} cell types identified', fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel('X coordinate', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Y coordinate', fontsize=12)

# Create a simplified legend (show top 10 types)
top_types = counts.head(10).index.tolist()
handles = []
labels = []
for celltype in top_types:
    handles.append(plt.Line2D([0], [0], marker='o', color='w', 
                             markerfacecolor=colors[celltype], markersize=8))
    labels.append(f"{celltype} ({counts[celltype]:,})")

if n_types > 10:
    handles.append(plt.Line2D([0], [0], marker='o', color='w', 
                             markerfacecolor='gray', markersize=8))
    labels.append(f"Other {n_types-10} types")

plt.legend(handles, labels, title='Cell Types (Top 10)', 
           bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left', fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Create a bar plot of cell type distribution
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
top_20_types = counts.head(20)
bars = plt.barh(range(len(top_20_types)), top_20_types.values)
plt.yticks(range(len(top_20_types)), top_20_types.index)
plt.xlabel('Number of Cells', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Cell Type', fontsize=12)
plt.title(f'Top 20 Cell Type Distribution (Total: {len(celltype_predictions):,} cells)', fontsize=14)

# Add count labels
for i, (bar, count) in enumerate(zip(bars, top_20_types.values)):
    width = bar.get_width()
    plt.text(width + 0.01*max(top_20_types.values), bar.get_y() + bar.get_height()/2,
             f'{count:,} ({count/len(celltype_predictions):.1%})',
             va='center', ha='left', fontsize=9)

plt.gca().invert_yaxis()  # Highest count at top
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

query_text: Can you identify the different cell populations here? I have a reference scRNA-seq dataset at './breast_cancer_reference.h5ad' if that helps.
